title: archidendropsis paivana subsp. tenuispica
intro: Archidendropsis paivana subsp. tenuispica is a plant species, also known as the 'Kahili Ginger', belonging to the family Fabaceae. The Kahili Ginger has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It has been reported to possess various health benefits, including pain relief, fever reduction, and improved digestion. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves of the Archidendropsis paivana subsp. tenuispica can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
